findings.finding_title: Ensure Amazon EKS control plane logging is enabled for all log types
findings.description: N/A
combined_recommendations: Enable Kubernetes API server component logs ("api") and audit logs ("audit") of the EKS control plane through the enabled_cluster_log_types attribute.
Enable logging for the EKS control plane

findings.finding_title: Ensure Amazon EKS public endpoint not accessible to 0.0.0.0/0
findings.description: N/A
combined_recommendations: Explicitly disable public endpoint access in the vpc_config resource of the EKS cluster.
Set the instance to not be publicly accessible
Don't enable public access to EKS Clusters

findings.finding_title: Ensure DynamoDB point in time recovery (backup) is enabled
findings.description: N/A

findings.finding_title: Ensure IAM policies does not allow write access without constraints
findings.description: N/A
combined_recommendations: Restrict IAM policies to specific resources to prevent data exfiltration actions.
Specify which user the permission should be used on or avoid using the listed actions.
Review IAM policies to ensure they do not allow resource exposure. Avoid actions like `ecr:SetRepositoryPolicy` and use alternatives that do not expose AWS resources.

findings.finding_title: Ensure Domain Name System Security Extensions (DNSSEC) signing is enabled for Amazon Route 53 public hosted zones
findings.description: N/A

findings.finding_title: Ensure that S3 bucket has a Public Access block
findings.description: N/A
combined_recommendations: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls
Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT
Enable blocking any PUT calls with a public ACL specified
Define a aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block for the given bucket to control public access policies
Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)
Add a logging block to the resource to enable access logging

findings.description: N/A

findings.finding_title: Can elevate its own privileges
findings.description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.
combined_recommendations: Specify seccomp either by annotation or by seccomp profile type having allowed values as per pod security standards
Specify at least one unneeded capability in 'containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop'

To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

findings.finding_title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop all
findings.description: The container should drop all default capabilities and add only those that are needed for its execution.
combined_recommendations: Set 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop' to 'ALL' and only add 'NET_BIND_SERVICE' to 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add'.

findings.finding_title: CPU not limited
findings.description: Enforcing CPU limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.

findings.finding_title: Image tag ":latest" used
findings.description: It is best to avoid using the ':latest' image tag when deploying containers in production. Doing so makes it hard to track which version of the image is running, and hard to roll back the version.
findings.recommendation: Use a specific container image tag that is not 'latest'.

findings.finding_title: Runs with UID <= 10000
findings.description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.

findings.finding_title: Workloads in the default namespace
findings.description: Checks whether a workload is running in the default namespace.

findings.finding_title: Instances in a subnet should not receive a public IP address by default.
findings.description: You should limit the provision of public IP addresses for resources. Resources should not be exposed on the public internet, but should have access limited to consumers required for the function of your application.
findings.recommendation: Set the instance to not be publicly accessible
combined_recommendations: Set the instance to not be publicly accessible
Set a more restrictive cidr range

findings.finding_title: VPC Flow Logs is a feature that enables you to capture information about the IP traffic going to and from network interfaces in your VPC. After you've created a flow log, you can view and retrieve its data in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. It is recommended that VPC Flow Logs be enabled for packet "Rejects" for VPCs.
findings.description: VPC Flow Logs provide visibility into network traffic that traverses the VPC and can be used to detect anomalous traffic or insight during security workflows.

findings.finding_title: AWS AMI data source should specify owners
findings.description: AWS AMI data source should specify owners to avoid using unverified AMIs. The owners field helps ensure you're using AMIs from known and trusted sources.
findings.recommendation: Specify the owners field in the AWS AMI data source configuration

findings.finding_title: ECR repository has image scans disabled.
findings.description: Repository image scans should be enabled to ensure vulnerable software can be discovered and remediated as soon as possible.
combined_recommendations: Set `image_tag_mutability` to IMMUTABLE in the ECR repository configuration.
Enable image scanning on push for the ECR repository to ensure vulnerabilities are detected early.

findings.finding_title: ECR images tags shouldn't be mutable.
findings.description: ECR images should be set to IMMUTABLE to prevent code injection through image mutation. This can be done by setting image_tag_mutability to IMMUTABLE
findings.recommendation: Only use immutable images in ECR

findings.finding_title: IAM Pass Role Filtering
findings.description: Ensures any IAM pass role attached to roles are flagged and warned.
findings.recommendation: Resolve permission escalations by denying pass role'

findings.finding_title: DynamoDB tables should use at rest encryption with a Customer Managed Key
findings.description: Using AWS managed keys does not allow for fine grained control. DynamoDB tables are encrypted by default using AWS managed encryption keys. To increase control of the encryption and control the management of factors like key rotation, use a Customer Managed Key.
findings.recommendation: Enable server side encryption with a customer managed key
combined_recommendations: Configure your own AWS KMS encryption key to protect your data in the DynamoDB table. You can either create a new aws_kms_key resource or use the ARN of an existing key in your AWS account.
Enable encryption using customer managed keys
Enable server side encryption with a customer managed key

findings.finding_title: Possible binding to all interfaces
findings.description: The code binds the application to all network interfaces, which may expose it to external access. CWE-605: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/605.html
findings.recommendation: Restrict the binding to specific interfaces or use a configuration setting to control the binding behavior. Refer to: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.8.6/plugins/b104_hardcoded_bind_all_interfaces.html

findings.finding_title: Hardcoded variable `TESTING` detected
findings.description: Hardcoded variable `TESTING` detected. Use environment variables or config files instead
findings.recommendation: Avoid hardcoding configuration variables like `TESTING`. Use environment variables or configuration files to manage such settings.

findings.finding_title: Avoid running Flask app with host 0.0.0.0
findings.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.
findings.recommendation: Configure the Flask app to use a more restrictive host, such as 127.0.0.1, to prevent public exposure.

findings.finding_title: Run container as non-root user
findings.description: When running containers in Kubernetes, it's important to ensure that they are properly secured to prevent privilege escalation attacks. One potential vulnerability is when a container is allowed to run applications as the root user, which could allow an attacker to gain access to sensitive resources. To mitigate this risk, it's recommended to add a `securityContext` to the container, with the parameter `runAsNonRoot` set to `true`. This will ensure that the container runs as a non-root user, limiting the damage that could be caused by any potential attacks. By adding a `securityContext` to the container in your Kubernetes pod, you can help to ensure that your containerized applications are more secure and less vulnerable to privilege escalation attacks.

findings.finding_title: EC2 launch template has Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1) enabled
findings.description: The EC2 launch template has Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1) enabled. IMDSv2 introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. You should either disable IMDS or require the use of IMDSv2.
findings.recommendation: Disable IMDS or require the use of IMDSv2 to improve security.
combined_recommendations: Disable IMDS or require the use of IMDSv2.
Disable IMDS or require the use of IMDSv2 to improve security.

findings.finding_title: Ensure IAM policies don't allow credentials exposure
findings.description: Credentials exposure actions return credentials as part of the API response, and can possibly lead to leaking important credentials. Instead, use another action that doesn't return sensitive data as part of the API response.
findings.recommendation: Review IAM policies to ensure they do not allow actions that return sensitive credentials in API responses. Use alternative actions that do not expose sensitive data.
combined_recommendations: Avoid using actions that can result in privilege escalation. Refer to the provided links for mitigation strategies.
Review IAM policies to ensure they do not allow actions that return sensitive credentials in API responses. Use alternative actions that do not expose sensitive data.
